warnings: Absolute pKa is highly sensitive to the G(H+,aq) reference (~1.4 unit shift between Tissandier 1998 and Kelly 2006). Prefer mode='reference' against a known acid in the same family.

Single-conformer result: every species was evaluated at ONE geometry, with no conformational/Boltzmann averaging. For a flexible molecule the Boltzmann-averaged free energy over conformers can differ from a single conformer by several kcal/mol — shifting pKa by >1 unit, E° by >0.1 V, and logP by >0.5. If the molecule has rotatable bonds, run conformer-search first and average, or treat this as a screening estimate tied to the input geometry.

prose:
Aqueous pKa of acetic acid (screening grade)

Method provenance:
Property: Absolute pKa (HA → A⁻ + H⁺) at 298 K, 1 atm.
Level of theory: PM7 semi-empirical Hamiltonian (MOPAC 22.1).
Geometry provenance: user-supplied protonated and deprotonated .xyz files were each re-optimized and frequency-analyzed at PM7 within the pka-acidity workflow (native MOPAC EF optimizer, gradient norm < 5×10⁻³ kcal mol⁻¹ Å⁻¹; no imaginary modes).
Solvent/environment: MOPAC implicit water (COSMO-type) in absolute mode; no explicit solvent molecules.
Charges & multiplicities: HA 0/1, A⁻ –1/1, H⁺ +1/1.
Software: MOPAC 22.1 via chemkit pka-acidity skill.
Convergence & caveats: both species optimized without SCF or geometry issues (integrity.trustworthy = true). Warnings from engine reproduced in full above. Single-conformer evaluation; PM7 absolute pKa is suitable only for rough screening, not quantitative prediction.

Live log: a complete run transcript was streamed to the working directory during execution (path reported at run time via chemkit).
